title: If you buy a new car, you begin to notice other same cars
html:
TL;DR

  • Mar 27 2021 Apple Note (modified Mar 30) from 19-year-old Yahir: a four-line aphorism cluster naming the frequency-illusion / Baader-Meinhof effect in his own vocabulary — "If you buy a new car, you begin to notice other same cars / If you're bald, you begin to see other bald people more / (Applies with opportunities/ business) / If you're around certain ideas and concepts, you begin to see them more and how they cross/ apply throughout."
  • Yahir naming the frequency illusion mechanism three years before he formalizes the concept page. Earliest dated articulation of attention-shapes-perception in the corpus.
  • The parenthetical "(Applies with opportunities/ business)" is the operationalization step — Yahir converts a perceptual quirk into an investing/entrepreneurship heuristic. Sibling move to the surface area of luck frame.
  • "Ideas and concepts, you begin to see them more and how they cross/ apply throughout" — proto-statement of the vault thesis itself: cross-pollination of concepts, the systems thinking move. The whole SecondBrain project is downstream of this 2021 line.
  • Form: 4-line manifesto style note, escalating from concrete (car, baldness) → abstract (opportunities) → meta (idea-cross-pollination). Classic ladder-of-abstraction in a 60-word note.

Source quote

"If you buy a new car, you begin to notice other same cars

If you're bald, you begin to see other bald people more

(Applies with opportunities/ business)

If you're around certain ideas and concepts, you begin to see them more and how they cross/ apply throughout"

Open questions

  • Did Yahir know the formal name "Baader-Meinhof phenomenon" / "frequency illusion" in 2021, or did he derive the pattern independently? The note's vernacular framing suggests independent derivation.
  • The line "around certain ideas and concepts, you begin to see them more" — is this the founding intuition behind the SecondBrain vault? Worth elevating into a synthesis page on attention-as-graph-density.
  • Applies with business — connect to specific 2021 ventures (Bromenu pre-launch?) where this heuristic was operative.

Wikilinks

frequency as belonging · surface area of luck · systems thinking · nonobvious · asking the right question · bromenu · do things that dont scale

related.label: Distinguishing your self doesn't mean getting ahead, bringing something to the table of value is what gets you ahead
